description_long: This is a thick, rectangular book with a white cover featuring a large, intricate circular diagram composed of geometric shapes and fine lines. The edges of the pages are visible and have a slightly aged, brownish tint, suggesting the book is well-used or designed to look antique. The spine is dark with a marbled black-and-white pattern, contrasting with the clean white of the cover. The book appears to be hardcover, with a sturdy, solid build. The cover design is highly detailed, with concentric circles and connecting lines, giving it a mystical or alchemical appearance. There are no visible illustrations apart from the geometric diagram, and the book exudes an aura of mystery and scholarly importance.
description: A thick, hardcover book with a white cover featuring a detailed circular geometric diagram and a marbled black-and-white spine. The page edges are brownish, giving it an antique look.
description_view_0: The book is seen from an angled top-down view, highlighting the intricate circular diagram on the cover and the marbled spine on the left.
description_view_1: A flat side view showing the full width of the book and the brownish page edges, with the circular diagram visible on the cover.
description_view_2: Another angled top-down view from the opposite side, again showing the cover's diagram and the marbled spine now on the right.
description_view_3: A direct view of the spine, displaying its marbled black-and-white pattern and the thickness of the book.
attribute_cover_illustration: The front cover features a large, highly detailed circular geometric diagram with multiple concentric rings, lines, and nodes, rendered in a precise, technical style. The illustration evokes themes of mysticism, alchemy, or esoteric knowledge. No other imagery or artwork is present.
attribute_title_text: THEORY & PRACTICE - F. HARTMAN - PARACELSUS - THEOPHRASTUS - PHILIPPUS - AUREOLUS - BOMBAST - VON - HOHENHEIM - (1493-1541)
attribute_spine_design: The spine is covered in a marbled black-and-white pattern with no visible text or decorative elements. The design is abstract and visually distinct from the plain cover.
